title: Subject-Verb Agreement
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Subject-Verb Agreement.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Compound subjects, Collective nouns rules, Prepositional phrase intercept traps (along with, in addition to).

content_sections.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
content_sections.content:
### Syntactic Alignment, Modifiers & Logical Transition Indicators
Verbal Aptitude studies grammatical structure, modifier bounds, and logical flow connections.

#### Subject-Verb Agreement with Intervening Phrases
Verbs must align with their subjects, not object nouns located in intervening prepositional phrases (e.g. "One of the servers *is* down," not "One of the servers *are* down").

#### Dangling Modifiers
A modifying phrase must refer to an active subject declared immediately after the phrase. If the subject is omitted, the modifier is left dangling:
*   *Dangling (Incorrect):* "While writing code, the system crashed."
*   *Resolved (Correct):* "While I was writing code, the system crashed."

#### Logical Transition categories
*   **Adversative:** Indicates contrast (e.g. *nevertheless*, *nonetheless*, *conversely*).
*   **Causal:** Indicates result (e.g. *therefore*, *hence*, *consequently*).

### Depth Analysis: Subject-Verb Agreement
In production configurations, we analyze how Compound subjects, Collective nouns rules, Prepositional phrase intercept traps (along with, in addition to) affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Subject-Verb Agreement
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Subject-Verb Agreement is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Subject-Verb Agreement
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Subject-Verb Agreement loops.
